tags.note: Although this is a public garden with public access, it has been closed for some time. I couldn't find any information as to why this is the case. It being a listed site, I presume this is to prevent vandalism or maybe due to lack of government funding.

tags.description: Burial place of Dorothy Wyndlow Pattison, better known as Sister Dora (16 January 1832 – 24 December 1878), an Anglican nun and nurse. Her burial place is surrounded by the graves of other Walsall dignitaries from the Victorian period.

tags.description: Garden and trail reflecting Jane Lane’s flight as she aided the escape of Charles II in the 1640′s. Totem poles have terracotta plaques reflecting the town at the time; flags reflect each point in his journey across the country.
